items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
items.content: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

items.title: Apple to produce Made in America wireless chips with Broadcom
items.content: Apple has signed a $30 billion+ multiyear deal with Broadcom to design and produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made custom wireless connectivity chips for Apple products.

items.title: Apple brings back card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after a four-year hiatus
items.content: Apple has started a phased rollout of card payments for Apple Account purchases in India after adapting to the country's payments framework.

items.title: Apple is reportedly planning new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro releases early next year
items.content: Apple is readying several new iPad Pro tablets and a budget-friendly MacBook Pro, reports suggest.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
items.content: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

items.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
items.content: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hi

items.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
items.content: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

items.title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
items.content: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.

items.title: Will Oil Prices Soar as the U.S.-Iran Truce Frays? The Answer Lies With China.
items.content: For decades, OPEC influenced the market by how much oil it produced. But China, the largest importer, is demonstrating its remarkable power over prices.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Traffic Falls to Lowest Point in a Month After Strikes
items.content: Shipping activity plummeted after Iran attacked a ship, setting off U.S. strikes. Little crude oil is being transported through the critical waterway.

items.title: Wall Street’s Big Week for Earnings and Economic Data
items.content: Earnings season kicks off in earnest on Tuesday with big banks up first. Corporate America faces a high bar to beat investor expectations.
